work.desc_title: Anonymous (? Abraham of Nathpar), Admonition.

excerpt.as_written: ܬܘܒ ܡܪܬܝܢܘܬܐ ܕܐܢܫ ܝܚܝܕܝܐ

excerpt.as_written: ... ܐܚ̈ܝ ܚܒܝܒܐ ܬܪܥܐ ܦܬܝܚ ܐܘܪܚܐ ܕܪܝܫܐ ܡܒܘܥܐ ܕܪܐ ܘܡܫܩܐ ܠܕܨܗܝ
ܘܥܒܪ ܡܢܟ ܟܠܗܝܢ ܨܢܥ̈ܬܐ ܘܡܚܫ̈ܒܬܐ ܒܝܫ̈ܬܐ ܘܟܕ ܡܢ ܟܠ ܚܛܗ̈ܝܢ ܢܪܚܩ ܢܣܩ ܫܘܒܚܐ ܠܐܒܐ ܘܠܒܪܐ ܘܠܪܘܚܐ ܕܩܘܕܫܐ ܠܥܠܡ ܥܠܡܝܢ ܐܡܝܢ

note: The text is made up of extracts from different works by Aphrahat and Abraham of Nathpar: Aphrahat, Demonstration 6.6 (ed. Parisot, Patrologia Syriaca I, col. 265 line 24 – 268 line 22) + Abraham of Nathpar, Discourse 6.5–7 (ed. Chahine, p. 262, line 9 – p. 263, line 7), Discourse 2.10,11 (ed. Chahine, p. 248, lines 4–8, line 14 – p. 249, line 3).

... ܘܡܫܟܚ ܟܠ ܚ̇ܕܐ ܗܘ ܒܦܘܢܝܟ
ܫܠܡ ܡܕܪ̈ܫܐ ܫܒ̈ܥܐ ܕܬܝܒܘܬܐ ܕܛܘܒܢܐ ܡܪܝ ܝܥܩܘܒ

translation: Madrāshē on virginity, to the melody ‘Bride of the King’, selected

work.desc_title: Patristic pro-Chalcedon florilegium.

excerpt.as_written: ܥܠ ܚܝܠܗ ܕܡܪܢ ܡܫܪܝܢܢ ܨܠܘܬܐ ܕܩܕܡ ܬܘܪܓܡܐ ܕܐܝܬܝܗ ܗܟܢܐ
translation: With the power of our Lord, we begin the prayer before the interpretation, which is thus

excerpt.as_written: ... ܡܢ ܩܕܝܡ ܟܠ ܡܕܡ ܘܒܬܪ ܟܠܡܕܡ ܘܥܠ ܐܦܝ ܟܠܡܕܡ ܘܚܠܦ ܟܠܡܕܡ
ܘܥܠ ܠܒܐ ܕܒܪܢܫܐ ܠܐ ܣܠܩ ܕܠܗܝܢ ܢܗܘܐ ܕܟܠܢ ܢܫܬܘܐ ܒܛܝܒܘܬܗ ܘܒܡܪܚܡܢܘܬܗ ܕܡܪܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܡܫܝܚܐ܆ ܕܒܐܝܕ̈ܘܗܝ ܘܥܡܗ ܬܫܒܘܚܬܐ

note: Florilegium features citations attributed to Severian of Gabala, John Chrysostom, Cyril of Alexandria, Jacob of Serugh, Ephrem of Nisibis, Basil of Caesarea, Isaac the Teacher, Athanasius of Alexandria, Proclus of Constantinople, Gregory of Nazianzus, Anastasius of Antioch.

work.desc_title: Tree of Porphyry and extracts from Aristotle’s Categories

excerpt.as_written: ܡܡܠܠܐ ܡܛܠ ܟܝ̈ܢܝܬܐ ܡܢ ܟܬܒܐ ܕܦܝܠܝܣܘܦܐ ܗܢܘ ܕܝܢ ܡܛܠ ܐܘܣܝܐ
translation: Discourse on natural things from the Book of the Philosopher, i.e. about substance

excerpt.as_written: ... ܐܘܣܝܐ ܡܬܦܠܓܐ ܠܓܘܫܡܐ ܘܠܠܐ ܓܘܫܡܐ
ܘܓܢܣܐ ܒܪܢܫܐ ܕܓܘܐ ܐܕܫܐ ܒܠܚܘܕ ܘܒܕܓܘܢ ܐܕܫ ܐܕ̈ܫܝܢ ...

note: The text comprises the so-called Tree of Porphyry (f. 208r, lines 3–14) and a brief exposition of the philosophical concept ‘substance’ that demonstrates similarities to the corresponding sections in the 3rd book of the (long) Commentary on Aristotle’s Categories by Sergius of Reshʿayna (ed. Y. Arzhanov, Sergius of Reshaina, Commentary on Aristotle’s ‘Categories’. Critical Edition and Translation (Philosophy and Sciences in the Christian Orient, 2), Berlin: De Gruyter, 2024): in particular, ch. 166 (ff. 208r–v) and ch. 169 (ff. 208v–209r). Identical text can be also found in British Library, Add. 17,201, ff. 7v–8v as part of theological florilegium.
